page: Amazon Web Services r7g.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton processor family. Utilizing the arm64 architecture with AWS Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz, this server features 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor configuration includes a 32 MB L3 cache and supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX or AMX. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The r7g.metal achieves top-tier results in multi-core Geekbench testing and software compilation using Clang, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 6185.1 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 253.87 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated strong, as are database operations and Redis SET operations, which reach 103.78 million operations per second. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, and LLM text generation using small models is a notable bottleneck, registering poor performance at 8.4 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7g.metal offers high resource density and high memory bandwidth per core, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 bare-metal instances for memory-intensive workloads. The absence of hyperthreading means each of the 64 cores is fully dedicated, avoiding resource contention. This hardware profile is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and software compilation pipelines. It is less suitable for workloads requiring local high-speed SSD storage, GPU acceleration, or single-threaded execution speed.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the ARM-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. Operating at 2.6 GHz on the arm64 architecture, it features 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and memory bandwidth, with strong results in database and Redis operations. However, single-core performance is average, and small-model LLM text generation is poor. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and concurrent compilation tasks, offering dedicated physical cores that eliminate hyperthreading resource contention.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated ARM-based Graviton physical cores, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores and 512 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive databases.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized ARM server featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and half a terabyte of high-speed DDR5 memory.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARM Neoverse-V1 cores with no hyperthreading.
512 GB of high-bandwidth DDR5 memory providing 8 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core compilation and memory bandwidth performance.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and concurrent compilation.
